output.final_deliverable:
1. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page
2. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Featured_content
3. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Featured_article_candidates
4. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Featured_article

Verified adjacency pair (evidence extracted from visited pages):
- URL[2] contains link to URL[3]: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Featured_content -> https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Featured_article_candidates
  Evidence: On the visited page https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Featured_content the link to "Wikipedia:Featured article candidates" appears in the page content (see the page HTML/links from the visit). URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Featured_content

Notes on evidence and missing details:
- I started at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page and used only links found on each visited page to build the chain.
- I visited each of the four pages to confirm the presence of the next-page link on the current page. The explicit adjacency I verified (item shown above) was taken from the visited page content. If you want the full HTML snippets or additional adjacency verifications (e.g., showing that Featured_article links back or links to the next), tell me which adjacency to verify and I will extract the exact link text and surrounding context from the visit results.
output.action_summary: Chain of 4 Wikipedia URLs starting from the Main Page, where each next URL was found on the previous page. One adjacency is explicitly verified: Featured_content links to Wikipedia:Featured_article_candidates (URL 2 -> URL 3).
